mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, weak labor demand signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in both series fail to produce a positive mean forward return or yield a Rank IC below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, creating short-term positive momentum for equity markets.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in 65% of recovery-phase observations or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 100 trades.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in 60% of high-inflation trigger months or the correlation between CPI spikes and negative returns drops below -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer expenditure, which directly supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 or shows no statistical edge over a random walk in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand growth across sectors, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative returns in 55% of cases or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and stagflation risks, which compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The dual signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance or produces positive returns in 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
